description: Manifest-bound schedule-aware analysis for Run 026 strict-threshold physical replay.

expected_headline.txa: TXA/N01: 16/16 schedule rows SEND; 504 received packets; mean delivered usefulness 0.538
expected_headline.txb: TXB/N16: 4/16 schedule rows SEND; 127 received packets; mean delivered usefulness 0.866
expected_headline.ratio: Observed received-packet ratio 0.2520; scheduled send-fraction ratio 0.2500.

interpretation: Run 026 uses a stricter usefulness-threshold schedule than Runs 024 and 025: TXB schedules 4/16 SEND rows rather than 8/16. The observed TXB/TXA received-packet ratio is consistent with the scheduled send-fraction ratio, while TXB retained higher mean delivered usefulness per received packet.
cautions: This is point-to-point LoRa at 915 MHz, not LoRaWAN.
The schedule CSVs define one repeated schedule period.
The analyzer compares schedule proportions and observed packet proportions.
The analyzer does not infer exact transmitted packet counts.
Missing sequence numbers are observed sequence gaps, not confirmed collisions.
recv_ms and tx_ms are not synchronized across boards and should not be interpreted as true latency.
Usefulness and priority are synthetic metadata in this milestone.
The run does not yet use a live belief-maintenance controller.
Use the wording reduced physical transmission attempts under scheduled skipping, not airtime optimization.
